payload.text: The 10–15 min rest on the hot tray is structural, not optional — protein cookies are too soft to handle straight from the oven and will firm to chewy as they cool. Judge texture only once fully cooled.
Brown butter goes from golden to burned in seconds — pull off the heat the moment the foam subsides and the milk solids smell nutty. Residual pan heat keeps cooking it, so pour it off immediately.

50g whey is high for this flour ratio — the dough will be thicker and drier than a standard cookie. If it crumbles rather than holds a mound, add milk a teaspoon at a time; don't be tempted to add more flour.
The 30-min chill is what makes thick centers possible — it re-solidifies the 15g of butter so the three mounds hold their height instead of spreading flat.

These stay pale — Splenda doesn't brown. Don't wait for golden color as your doneness cue. Pull at 9–11 min when the edges are set and the center still looks underdone; carryover heat on the hot tray finishes them.
